page: Amazon Web Services z1d.12xlarge is a high-frequency, memory-optimized instance built on the AWS Nitro System. It features 48 vCPUs, corresponding to 24 physical cores with 2 threads per core, utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The instance is powered by an Intel Xeon 8151 processor running at a sustained core speed of 4.0 GHz, supported by a 24 MB L3 cache. It includes hardware-level features such as Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX and AVX-512F), AES cryptography, and Intel Turbo Boost technology.

The hardware configuration is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. For local storage, the z1d.12xlarge includes 1800 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes, offering high-speed, low-latency local storage directly attached to the hypervisor. Network connectivity is delivered via a baseline bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong memory performance, with large-block read-only memory bandwidth reaching 215.38 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 92.12 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25% tier. CPU-bound tasks, such as stress-ng multi-core performance (52390.69) and Passmark CPU scores (44393.51), fall within the average range. However, LLM text generation benchmarks show weaker performance, indicating that this instance is not optimized for large language model inference without GPU acceleration. From a resource density perspective, the combination of high-frequency dedicated CPU cores, substantial DDR4 memory, and integrated NVMe storage provides a balanced profile for workloads requiring high single-threaded performance alongside fast local storage.

Given the high clock speed, large memory footprint, and fast local NVMe storage, the z1d.12xlarge is suited for relational databases with high per-core licensing costs, electronic design automation (EDA), financial simulations, and data-intensive workloads that benefit from high single-core compute performance and low-latency storage.
description: Amazon Web Services z1d.12xlarge is a high-frequency, memory-optimized instance built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 48 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8151 processor running at 4.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 1800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, alongside a 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data highlights strong memory performance, with large-block read-only bandwidth reaching 215.38 GB/sec, while CPU and database operations perform in the average tier. The instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that require high single-core compute speeds combined with high memory density and fast local storage. It is well-suited for relational databases, electronic design automation (EDA), and financial simulation workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services z1d.12xlarge is an x86_64 instance featuring 48 vCPUs at 4.0 GHz, 384 GB DDR4 RAM, and 1800 GB local NVMe SSD storage, optimized for high-frequency compute and database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services z1d.12xlarge features 48 vCPUs at 4.0 GHz, 384 GB DDR4 RAM, and 1800 GB local NVMe SSD storage for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A high-frequency compute and memory-optimized platform featuring dedicated 4.0 GHz cores and integrated local NVMe SSD storage.
bullet_points: 48 vCPUs powered by high-frequency 4.0 GHz Intel Xeon processors
384 GB of DDR4 memory providing 8.0 GB per physical core
1800 GB of integrated local NVMe SSD instance storage
Strong memory bandwidth performance exceeding 215 GB/sec
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via the Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for relational databases, EDA, and financial simulations
